description: Fix documentation inaccuracies found during a comprehensive audit of the docs site against the current codebase. Covers incorrect API signatures, wrong CLI flag references, stale example output, missing features in docs, and outdated SVG illustration text.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix plugin-development guide fetch utility grouping and add SDK reference link
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want accurate fetch utility documentation so that I use the correct function signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx, the parenthetical '(same for putJSON, patchJSON, deleteJSON)' on the postJSON line is corrected to '(same for putJSON, patchJSON)' — deleteJSON is NOT in the body-parameter group
A separate line or note clarifies that deleteJSON follows the fetchJSON pattern (url, init?, schema?) without a body parameter

The SDK Utilities section includes a note or link directing readers to the full SDK Reference (sdk/utilities) for the complete list of utilities, so that the incomplete inline listing is not misleading
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx. Around lines 303-304, there's a description of fetch utilities that groups deleteJSON with postJSON/putJSON/patchJSON as body-parameter methods. The actual deleteJSON signature is (url, init?, schema?) — same as fetchJSON, no body parameter. Fix the grouping and add a cross-reference to docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx for the complete utility list. The guide currently lists only a subset of SDK utilities (missing fetchFromPage, waitForSelectorRemoval, querySelectorAll, observeDOM, getCurrentUrl, getPageTitle, several storage functions, sleep, etc.) — rather than enumerating them all here, add a link to the SDK reference page.

userStories.title: Fix resource read() signature in resources-prompts guide
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want correct resource definition examples so that my read() handler compiles.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx, the typed resource example's read() method includes the required uri parameter: async read(uri: string): Promise<ResourceContent>
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx. Around line 134, the second resource example (typed resources) shows `async read(): Promise<ResourceContent>` without the uri parameter. The ResourceDefinition interface in platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts line 73 requires `read(uri: string): Promise<ResourceContent>`. Add the uri parameter to the example.

acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx, the opentabs doctor example output shows a Node.js version >= 22 (e.g., v22.11.0), not v20.11.0
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/guides/logging-debugging.mdx. Around line 211, the example opentabs doctor output shows 'Runtime: Node.js v20.11.0'. The minimum requirement is Node.js 22+ (engines.node >= 22.0.0 in root package.json). Change to something like 'Runtime: Node.js v22.11.0'.

userStories.title: Fix CLI reference — add Windsurf to doctor checks and fix --show-secret description
userStories.description: As a user, I want the CLI reference to accurately list all doctor checks and flag descriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx, the doctor checks list includes Windsurf alongside Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenCode for MCP client config checking
The --show-secret flag description for 'opentabs config show' mentions that it also displays MCP client configurations, not just the secret
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx. (1) Around line 243, the doctor check description says it checks 'Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenCode MCP configuration files'. The source (platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts, defaultMcpClientLocations()) also checks Windsurf (~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json). Add Windsurf to the list. (2) Around line 559, --show-secret says 'Display the full authentication secret instead of masking it'. The actual Commander description is 'Display the full authentication secret and MCP client configurations'. Update to match.

userStories.title: Fix configuration reference — correct --dev and --dangerously-skip-confirmation descriptions
userStories.description: As a user, I want accurate descriptions of environment variables and config options so I don't try nonexistent CLI flags.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx, the OPENTABS_DEV description does NOT say 'Equivalent to the --dev CLI flag' — instead it says something like 'Equivalent to passing --dev to the MCP server binary (used by platform contributors via npm run dev)'

The skipConfirmation section does NOT describe --dangerously-skip-confirmation as a 'CLI flag' — instead clarifies it is a flag on the MCP server binary (not the opentabs CLI) or recommends using the OPENTABS_SKIP_CONFIRMATION env var instead
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx. (1) Around line 283, OPENTABS_DEV description says 'Equivalent to the --dev CLI flag'. The --dev flag does NOT exist on the opentabs CLI (opentabs start has only --port and --show-config). The --dev flag is on the MCP server binary (node dist/index.js --dev), used only by contributors. Fix the description. (2) Around line 243, the skipConfirmation section says 'the --dangerously-skip-confirmation CLI flag'. This flag is NOT on the opentabs CLI — it's parsed directly from process.argv by the MCP server code (platform/mcp-server/src/skip-confirmation.ts). Running 'opentabs start --dangerously-skip-confirmation' would error. Fix to clarify it's a server binary flag or recommend the env var.

userStories.title: Fix MCP server reference — lastPollAt type, --show-secret, extension.reload type
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want accurate MCP server documentation so that API consumers use the correct types and commands.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx, the health endpoint JSON example shows lastPollAt as a number (e.g., 1740134100000) or null, NOT as an ISO 8601 string
The secret extraction command uses 'opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret' (with --show-secret flag)
In the WebSocket protocol table, extension.reload is listed as type 'Notification' (not 'Request')
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx. Three fixes: (1) Around line 178, the health endpoint example shows '"lastPollAt": "2026-02-21T10:30:00.000Z"' but the source (platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts line 315) sends state.fileWatching.mtimeLastPollAt which is typed as number | null in state.ts line 55. Change to a number like 1740134100000 or null. (2) Around line 599, the command 'opentabs config show --json | jq -r .secret' is missing --show-secret. Without it, the secret is masked via maskSecret() (platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts line 54). Add --show-secret. (3) Around line 429, the wire protocol table lists extension.reload as 'Request'. In the source (platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts lines 505-509), sendExtensionReload sends { jsonrpc: '2.0', method: 'extension.reload' } with no id field, making it a Notification. Change type to 'Notification'.

userStories.title: Fix troubleshooting page — add --show-secret to curl command
userStories.description: As a user, I want troubleshooting commands that actually work so I can diagnose plugin reload issues.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx, the curl command for manual plugin reload uses 'opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret' (with --show-secret flag)
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx. Around lines 124-125, the curl command for manual /reload uses '$(opentabs config show --json | jq -r .secret)'. Without --show-secret, the secret is masked (platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts line 54 calls maskSecret()). The curl would send a masked secret and get a 401. Change to '$(opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret)'.

userStories.title: Fix dev-setup page — correct build step and add format:check to quality checks
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want accurate dev setup instructions so I run the right build and verification commands.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx, the build pipeline step 1 says 'tsc --build' (not 'tsc --build --force') for the default npm run build command, with a note that build:force uses --force
The quality checks list includes 'npm run format:check' (Prettier formatting check) between lint and knip
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx. (1) Around line 31, the build pipeline description says step 1 is 'tsc --build --force'. The actual npm run build script in root package.json line 19 uses 'tsc --build' (incremental, not forced). Only 'npm run build:force' uses --force. Change to 'tsc --build' and note that build:force adds --force. (2) Around lines 143-153, the quality checks list has 6 commands but is missing 'npm run format:check'. The root package.json 'check' script (line 28) includes format:check: 'npm run build && npm run lint && npm run format:check && npm run knip && npm run test'. Add 'npm run format:check  # Prettier formatting check' between lint and knip.

userStories.title: Fix architecture page — correct OPENTABS_PORT env var description
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the architecture docs to accurately describe which env vars the MCP server reads directly.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx, the description of how the MCP server port is configured clarifies that the server reads PORT (not OPENTABS_PORT directly), and that OPENTABS_PORT is a CLI-level convenience env var that the opentabs CLI translates to PORT when spawning the server
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx. Around line 13, the doc says the MCP server is 'configurable via PORT or OPENTABS_PORT env var'. The MCP server (platform/mcp-server/src/index.ts lines 170-178) only reads process.env['PORT']. OPENTABS_PORT is handled by the CLI (platform/cli/src/parse-port.ts) which translates it to PORT when spawning the server process. The architecture page should say the server reads PORT, and that the CLI supports OPENTABS_PORT as a user-facing convenience.

userStories.title: Fix SVG illustrations — PluginStructure, LifecycleSequence, and ErrorCategories
userStories.description: As a docs reader, I want SVG illustrations with accurate API signatures and parameter names.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/components/illustrations.tsx, the PluginStructure illustration shows 'defineTool({ name, description, input, output, handle })' (all 5 required properties) instead of 'defineTool({ name, description, input, ... })'
The LifecycleSequence illustration shows 'onToolInvocationEnd(toolName, success, durationMs)' instead of 'onToolInvocationEnd(toolName, success, ms)'

The ErrorCategories illustration is consistent in showing or omitting the optional code? parameter across all factory methods — either all show it or none do (to match the existing notFound which shows code?, add code? to auth, validation, internal, timeout, and rateLimited)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/components/illustrations.tsx. Three fixes in this file:

(1) PluginStructure — around line 3237, change the text from 'defineTool({ name, description, input, ... })' to 'defineTool({ name, description, input, output, handle })'. The ToolDefinition interface (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:161-179) has 5 required properties: name, description, input, output, handle. The illustration should show all of them.

(2) LifecycleSequence — around line 1989, change 'onToolInvocationEnd(toolName, success, ms)' to 'onToolInvocationEnd(toolName, success, durationMs)'. The SDK source (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:272) uses 'durationMs' not 'ms'.

(3) ErrorCategories — around lines 2161, 2247, 2290, 2337-2346, 2400. The notFound factory at line 2204 shows 'ToolError.notFound(msg, code?)' but the other factories omit code?. For consistency, add code? to all:
- Line 2161: 'ToolError.auth(msg)' → 'ToolError.auth(msg, code?)'
- Line 2247: 'ToolError.validation(msg)' → 'ToolError.validation(msg, code?)'
- Line 2290: 'ToolError.internal(msg)' → 'ToolError.internal(msg, code?)'
- Lines 2337-2346: 'ToolError.rateLimited(msg, retryAfterMs?)' → 'ToolError.rateLimited(msg, retryAfterMs?, code?)'
- Line 2400: 'ToolError.timeout(msg)' → 'ToolError.timeout(msg, code?)'

The actual source signatures (platform/plugin-sdk/src/errors.ts) confirm all factory methods accept an optional code? parameter.

userStories.title: Fix publishing page — correct publishConfig claim
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want accurate publishing documentation so I understand which packages have publishConfig.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx, the statement about publishConfig accurately reflects that browser-extension does not have this field — either by saying 'most packages' or by listing the ones that do/don't
The page's lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx. Around line 99, the doc states 'All packages use publishConfig: { access: restricted }'. In reality, 6 of 7 packages have this field. The browser-extension package (platform/browser-extension/package.json) does NOT have publishConfig. Change the wording to something like 'All packages except browser-extension use publishConfig: { access: "restricted" }' or 'Most packages use publishConfig...'.
